title: Additivity Results for the Rényi-2 Entanglement of Purification
abstract: We reformulate the Rényi entanglement of purification as a constrained minimum output Rényi entropy problem. Equivalently, for $p>1$, this formulation can be expressed in terms of a constrained maximal output Schatten $p$-norm. More precisely, for a completely positive map $Ω:L(B')\to L(A)$, we consider the quantity $\upsilon_p(Ω)$ defined by optimizing $\|(Ω\otimes \mathrm{id}_E)(σ^{B'E})\|_p$ over all bipartite states $σ^{B'E}$ whose $B'$-marginal is maximally mixed. We focus on the case $p=2$. First, we compute $\upsilon_2$ for the transpose-depolarizing channel and prove that it is multiplicative under tensor powers. We then establish a general multiplicativity criterion: whenever a completely positive map $N:L(B')\to L(A)$ satisfies $N^{\dagger} \mathbin{\circ} N=a\,\mathrm{id}_A+b\,\mathrm{Tr}[\cdot]\,I_d$ for some constants $a,b\ge 0$, where $N^{\dagger}$ denotes the Hilbert-Schmidt adjoint of $N$, the quantity $\upsilon_2(N)$ is multiplicative under tensor powers. Examples of channels satisfying this criterion include the transpose-depolarizing channel, the depolarizing channel, and their respective complementary channels. Furthermore, we show that, for every completely positive map $Ω$, multiplicativity of $\upsilon_p(Ω)$ implies multiplicativity for its complementary map. This yields the corresponding additivity statements for the associated Rényi-2 entanglement of purification.
summary: This paper rigorously analyzes the additivity and multiplicativity of the Rényi-2 entanglement of purification for quantum channels. By reformulating the entanglement measure as a constrained Schatten norm optimization, the authors derive a general criterion that guarantees multiplicativity for certain classes of channels, proving this property for the important transpose-depolarizing channel.
main_problem:
The paper determines the additivity/multiplicativity properties of the Rényi-2 entanglement of purification, $
u_2(\Omega)$, for various quantum channels.
main_result:
The entanglement measure $
u_2(\Gamma^{\otimes n}_t)$ is proven to be multiplicative, $
u_2(\Gamma^{\otimes n}_t) = (
u_2(\Gamma_t))^n$, and a general criterion for this multiplicativity is established for CP maps.
method: The analysis reformulates the entanglement measure as a constrained maximum output Schatten $p$-norm problem, utilizing techniques involving the Choi-Jamiołkowski isomorphism and quadratic forms.
model_or_system: The study focuses on quantum channels, particularly the transpose-depolarizing channel $\Gamma_t$, and its tensor powers acting on bipartite quantum states.
key_observables:
The Rényi-2 entanglement of purification, $
u_2(\Omega)$, and the associated output Schatten 2-norm, $\upsilon_2(\Omega)$.
important_parameters: The Rényi parameter $p$ (focusing on $p=2$), the dimension $d$, and the channel parameter $t$.
main_assumptions: The derivation relies on reducing the optimization problem to extreme points and establishing structural properties of the channels (e.g., the criterion $N^\dagger \circ N = a 	ext{id}_A + b 	ext{Tr}[\cdot]	ext{Id}$).
paper_structure: The paper proceeds by first reformulating the entanglement measure, calculating the value for the specific channel $\Gamma_t$ (establishing bounds and a closed form), and then generalizing the result by proving the multiplicativity property for tensor powers and establishing the general criterion for additivity.
why_interesting: The results provide concrete, general mathematical criteria for when entanglement measures exhibit simple scaling (multiplicativity) under tensor products, which is crucial for understanding resource theories in quantum information.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
